message:
Ever watched your dog finish their business and then suddenly go full bulldozer — kicking up grass and dirt with their back legs like they're trying to bury something? 🐶💨 Here's the surprising part: they're not actually trying to cover it up at all. They're doing the exact opposite — making a big, bold announcement.

Dogs have special scent glands tucked between the pads of their paws. When they scratch and kick at the ground, they're stamping their own personal perfume right next to their poop — and those paw pheromones linger far longer than the smell of the poop itself. Add in the freshly torn-up grass as a visible "look over here" flag, and your dog has basically left a billboard that says: "I was here, and this spot is MINE." 🪧

So that goofy, dramatic grass-kick isn't bad manners or a mess in the making — it's an ancient, sophisticated way dogs have always talked to each other, long before they ever curled up on our couches. 😄

Does YOUR dog do the big dramatic kick after they go? Comment 🐾 if you've got a little lawn bulldozer at home!
